examples.title: Address Translation using Page Table
examples.description: Demonstrates virtual address to physical address translation
examples.code:
FUNCTION Translate(virtual_address, page_table)
BEGIN
    // Extract page number and offset from virtual address
    page_number = virtual_address / PAGE_SIZE
    offset = virtual_address % PAGE_SIZE
    
    // Check if page is valid in page table
    IF page_table[page_number].valid == FALSE THEN
        RETURN NULL  // Page fault will occur
    END IF
    
    // Get physical frame number
    frame_number = page_table[page_number].frame_number
    
    // Construct physical address
    physical_address = (frame_number * PAGE_SIZE) + offset
    
    RETURN physical_address
END
examples.explanation: This pseudocode shows the basic address translation process: extracting page number and offset from virtual address, checking validity in page table, retrieving frame number, and constructing physical address.

examples.title: Two-Level Page Table Lookup
examples.description: Implements two-level hierarchical page table lookup
examples.code:
PAGE_BITS = 10  # Bits for each level
OFFSET_BITS = 12   # 4KB page size
PTE_SIZE = 4       # Bytes per PTE

class PageTableEntry:
    def __init__(self, valid=False, frame=0, prot=7):
        self.valid = valid
        self.frame_number = frame
        self.protection = prot
        self.referenced = False
        self.dirty = False

def translate_two_level(va, outer_table):
    """Translate using two-level page table"""
    # Extract indices from 32-bit VA
    offset = va & ((1 << OFFSET_BITS) - 1)
    inner_index = (va >> OFFSET_BITS) & ((1 << PAGE_BITS) - 1)
    outer_index = (va >> (OFFSET_BITS + PAGE_BITS)) & ((1 << PAGE_BITS) - 1)
    
    # Level 1: Get inner page table base
    outer_pte = outer_table[outer_index]
    if not outer_pte.valid:
        raise PageFaultException("Invalid outer page table")
    
    inner_table_base = outer_pte.frame_number * (1 << OFFSET_BITS)
    
    # Level 2: Get frame number
    inner_pte_addr = inner_table_base + (inner_index * PTE_SIZE)
    inner_pte = read_memory(inner_pte_addr)  # Simulated
    
    if not inner_pte.valid:
        raise PageFaultException("Page not in memory")
    
    # Construct physical address
    frame_num = inner_pte.frame_number
    pa = (frame_num << OFFSET_BITS) | offset
    
    # Update reference bit
    inner_pte.referenced = True
    
    return pa

# Example usage
outer_page_table = [PageTableEntry(valid=False) for _ in range(1024)]
# Set up valid entries...
virtual_address = 0x12345678
try:
    physical = translate_two_level(virtual_address, outer_page_table)
    print(f"VA: 0x{virtual_address:08x} -> PA: 0x{physical:08x}")
except PageFaultException as e:
    print(f"Page fault: {e}")
examples.explanation: This Python code demonstrates two-level page table lookup, showing how virtual address bits are divided among outer and inner table indices, with proper error handling for page faults.

examples.title: Page Table Entry Structure and Translation
examples.description: C structure defining PTE and address translation function
examples.explanation: This C code shows how page table entries are structured at the bit level, demonstrating efficient packing of translation and status information, with functions for address translation and page fault detection.
examples.spaceComplexity: O(2^20 * sizeof(PTE)) bytes = 4MB for full table

examples.title: TLB Lookup Algorithm
examples.description: Demonstrates TLB miss and hit handling in address translation
examples.code:
FUNCTION Translate_With_TLB(virtual_address, page_table, tlb)
BEGIN
    page_number = virtual_address / PAGE_SIZE
    offset = virtual_address % PAGE_SIZE
    
    // Step 1: Check TLB first
    tlb_entry = TLB_Lookup(tlb, page_number)
    
    IF tlb_entry IS NOT NULL THEN
        // TLB Hit - fast path
        frame_number = tlb_entry.frame_number
        physical_address = (frame_number * PAGE_SIZE) + offset
        RETURN physical_address
    END IF
    
    // Step 2: TLB Miss - lookup page table
    pte = page_table[page_number]
    
    IF pte.valid == FALSE THEN
        TRAP PageFault  // Handle page fault
    END IF
    
    // Step 3: Update TLB with new translation
    TLB_Insert(tlb, page_number, pte.frame_number)
    
    // Step 4: Complete translation
    physical_address = (pte.frame_number * PAGE_SIZE) + offset
    RETURN physical_address
END

FUNCTION TLB_Lookup(tlb, vpn)
BEGIN
    FOR each entry IN tlb DO
        IF entry.valid == TRUE AND entry.virtual_page == vpn THEN
            entry.referenced = TRUE  // Update usage
            RETURN entry
        END IF
    END FOR
    RETURN NULL  // TLB miss
END

FUNCTION TLB_Insert(tlb, vpn, pfn)
BEGIN
    // Find victim using replacement policy (e.g., LRU, FIFO)
    victim = TLB_Select_Victim(tlb)
    
    // Write back if dirty
    IF victim.dirty == TRUE THEN
        // Write back to memory
    END IF
    
    // Insert new entry
    victim.virtual_page = vpn
    victim.frame_number = pfn
    victim.valid = TRUE
    victim.dirty = FALSE
END
examples.explanation: This pseudocode demonstrates the TLB-assisted address translation process, showing the fast path on TLB hit, page table lookup on miss, and TLB update mechanisms including victim selection for replacement.
examples.timeComplexity: O(1) average (O(n) worst case for TLB lookup, O(1) for associative)
